evals.prompt: List all the Browzer workspaces in my organization.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes workspace-management and runs `browzer workspace list --json --save <file>`.
expectations: The workspace-management skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace list --json --save <file>`
Agent Read the saved file and presented the workspaces

evals.prompt: Show me the full JSON shape of workspace ws_abc123 — I want to know what fields are on it.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes workspace-management for zero-shot schema discovery via `browzer workspace get` with `--save` (note: `--json` flag does NOT exist on this subcommand).
expectations: The workspace-management skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace get ws_abc123 --save <file>` (without `--json` — that flag does not exist on `workspace get`)
Agent Read the file and reported the field shape

evals.expected_output: Agent invokes workspace-management, lists to find the id, confirms with the user, then deletes.
expectations: The workspace-management skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace list --json` first to find the id
Agent confirmed with the user BEFORE running `browzer workspace delete <id>`
Agent did NOT skip the confirmation step

evals.prompt: browzer init left an orphan workspace on the server. Clean it up.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes workspace-management, lists workspaces, cross-references with local `.browzer/config.json` to identify the orphan, confirms with the user, then deletes. If no orphan exists in the current environment, agent demonstrates the full diagnostic process anyway.
expectations: The workspace-management skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace list --json` to enumerate workspaces
Agent cross-referenced the list with local `.browzer/config.json` to identify the orphan (or report none found)
Agent explained the delete confirmation step (`--confirm-name` required in non-interactive shells)

evals.prompt: Am I logged in to Browzer?
evals.expected_output: Agent routes to auth-status (probe), NOT workspace-management.
expectations: The auth-status skill was invoked, NOT workspace-management
Agent ran `browzer status --json`, not `browzer workspace list`

evals.prompt: I'm reorganizing my local ~/projects directory and moving this repo from ~/projects/rag to ~/code/browzer-rag. The server-side workspace with its indexed code graph and embedded Langfuse docs must stay intact — I just want to stop the old path from being associated with it and associate the new path instead. Walk me through the exact command sequence and convince me delete is the wrong choice here.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes workspace-management, explains unlink is local-only while delete is destructive, runs unlink at the old path, then relink at the new path.
expectations: The workspace-management skill was invoked
Agent explained the distinction: `unlink` is local-only, `delete` destroys server data
Agent ran `browzer workspace unlink` from the old path
Agent ran `browzer workspace relink <id>` from the new path
Agent explicitly advised AGAINST `browzer workspace delete`

evals.prompt: A teammate created workspace ws_prod_api via `browzer init` on her machine. I just cloned the repo fresh on mine and I need my local clone to talk to the same server-side workspace — no re-indexing, no duplicate workspace, no losing her embedded Langfuse docs. Give me the exact command and tell me how to verify afterwards that I'm pointing at ws_prod_api and not a new workspace.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes workspace-management, runs `browzer workspace relink ws_prod_api`, verifies via `browzer status --json`, and confirms no new workspace was created.
expectations: The workspace-management skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace relink ws_prod_api`
Agent verified the binding with `browzer status --json` afterwards
Agent did NOT run `browzer init` (which would create a new workspace)
Agent explained how to confirm the workspace id in the status output matches ws_prod_api

evals.prompt: Fresh repo, nothing set up. I need this project initialized as a brand-new browzer workspace with its full code graph indexed and verified. Take it from zero.
evals.expected_output: Agent routes to embed-workspace-graphs (init + indexing), NOT workspace-management (which manages existing workspaces).
expectations: The embed-workspace-graphs skill was invoked, NOT workspace-management
Agent ran `browzer init --dry-run` before the real init
Agent ran `browzer init`
Agent verified with `browzer status --json` afterwards

evals.prompt: I only remember the workspace was named something like 'gestao-api' or 'gestao_api' — I'm not sure which. I need to see its full details (owner, created_at, chunk count) to figure out whether it's the one my previous team left behind, and also whether any teammate is currently linked to it.
evals.expected_output: Agent lists workspaces with optional `--filter gestao` to resolve name, then runs `workspace get` with `--save` (not `--json`). If no match exists, clearly reports the empty result.
expectations: The workspace-management skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace list --json --save <file>` (optionally with `--filter gestao`) to resolve the id
Agent Read the saved file and resolved the name to a concrete id, OR clearly reported no workspace matched that name
Agent ran `browzer workspace get <resolved-id> --save <file>` on the matched id (using `--save`, NOT `--json`)
Agent did NOT guess an id or pass the name string directly to `workspace get`
